decisions.briefing: Markets dipped modestly today — NASDAQ off 0.8% and S&P 500 off 0.5% — a mild risk-off session with no major catalyst. Portfolio sits at a strong +30% overall, but cash has dwindled to $298, well below the $500 deployment threshold, so no new positions can be opened today. All 7 current holdings remain within normal ranges; holding steady and waiting for cash to replenish through a stop-loss or profit-target exit before redeploying into top candidates like POET, NBIX, or INCY.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities retreated modestly on June 3rd, with the NASDAQ falling 0.8% and S&P 500 dropping 0.5%, reflecting a cautious, neutral tone across markets. The mild pullback came amid lingering uncertainty around interest rate policy and mixed economic signals, keeping institutional investors on the sidelines. Tech-heavy indices underperformed slightly, consistent with rotation away from growth names on risk-off days.
market_education.summary_zh: 美股6月3日小幅回落，那斯達克指數下跌0.8%，標普500指數收跌0.5%，市場整體情緒偏中性謹慎。市場走弱主要受到利率政策不確定性及經濟數據參差不齊的影響，機構投資者觀望情緒濃厚。科技股偏重的指數表現略遜大市，與風險規避日資金撤出成長型板塊的規律一致。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Dry powder refers to cash or liquid assets held in reserve so an investor can act quickly when a good opportunity appears. Today's situation is a perfect example of its importance: three portfolio slots are open and top-scoring candidates like POET and NBIX are flashing strong buy signals, but with only $298 in cash we can't pull the trigger. Maintaining a healthy cash cushion — typically 5–15% of portfolio value — ensures you're never forced to watch opportunities pass by.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「備用現金」（Dry Powder）指投資人預留的現金或流動資產，以便在好機會出現時能迅速出手。今天的情況正是最佳示例：組合有三個空位，POET、NBIX等強勢候選股正發出買入訊號，但現金僅剩298美元，無法執行任何買單。保持適當的現金比例（通常為組合總值的5至15%），是確保不錯失良機的關鍵紀律之一。
